w:r.w:t: Design direction: Soft purple (#7F77DD) + white — calm, trustworthy, feminine.
Home screen: Map-first, like Uber idle state. Map always visible, bottom sheet slides up.
Target users: Women and vulnerable individuals in Canada walking alone.
This document is the single source of truth. Build everything described here before shipping.

SafeWalk is a personal safety web application that monitors walking sessions and automatically alerts trusted contacts if something goes wrong. The web version is the first deliverable — it proves the product before moving to native mobile apps.

The user opens the app, sets up trusted contacts, then taps Start Walk. The app tracks their location in real time, shows a check-in countdown, and escalates through a 5-stage ladder (prompt, SMS to contacts, voice call, 911 option, ongoing monitoring) if the user stops responding. A trusted contact who receives an SMS gets a time-limited web link showing the user's live location on a map — no login required.
Key principle: This is a safety app. Every screen must load fast, work one-handed, and never feel confusing under stress. Clarity beats cleverness everywhere.
The MVP is complete when all of the following are true:
A new user can sign up, verify their email, complete onboarding, add a trusted contact, start a walk, trigger SOS, and end the walk — end to end, no errors.
All data (contacts, walk sessions, location history) is stored in Supabase, not localStorage.
Row Level Security is enabled on every table so users can only see their own data.
The trusted contact web view works on mobile browsers without login.
Auth flows (sign up, sign in, reset password, email confirmation) all work correctly.
The app passes Lighthouse accessibility audit with no critical failures.
PIPEDA compliance controls are visible in Settings (data export, delete, privacy policy).
Use exactly this stack. Do not substitute alternatives.
Walk session, auth state, UI state
Interactive map, route display, user pin
Validation on auth and contact forms
Deploy from GitHub, env vars, edge network
Scaffold the project with this exact folder structure:
│ │ ├── ui/ # Reusable: Button, Input, Badge, Avatar, Modal, Toast
│ │ ├── alertService.ts # trigger SMS/calls via Edge Functions
IMPORTANT: Set up Supabase completely before building any UI. Auth must work, RLS must be enabled,
and all tables must exist before you wire up any page. Building UI against localStorage first and
migrating later causes double the work and subtle bugs.
Create a .env.local file at the project root with these variables. Never commit this file to Git.
Run all migrations in order. Each table below must be created with the columns and RLS policies described.
Created automatically when a new user signs up via a Supabase trigger. Stores display name and phone number that are not part of auth.users.
id UUID REFERENCES auth.users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE PRIMARY KEY,
avatar_initials TEXT, -- e.g. "AJ" derived from full_name
FOR EACH ROW EXECUTE FUNCTION handle_new_user();
ALTER TABLE profiles ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
CREATE POLICY "Users can view own profile" ON profiles FOR SELECT USING (auth.uid() = id);
CREATE POLICY "Users can update own profile" ON profiles FOR UPDATE USING (auth.uid() = id);
Up to 5 trusted contacts per user. The is_primary flag designates the first contact who will be called (not just texted) during escalation Stage 3.
id UUID DEFAULT gen_random_uuid() PRIMARY KEY,
user_id UUID REFERENCES auth.users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE NOT NULL,
ALTER TABLE trusted_contacts ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
CREATE POLICY "Users manage own contacts" ON trusted_contacts
One row per walk. Status transitions: active -> completed or active -> sos_triggered. The share_token is a UUID used to build the trusted contact web view URL.
id UUID DEFAULT gen_random_uuid() PRIMARY KEY,
user_id UUID REFERENCES auth.users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE NOT NULL,
share_expires_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW() + INTERVAL '24 hours'
ALTER TABLE walk_sessions ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
CREATE POLICY "Users manage own sessions" ON walk_sessions
-- Public read via share_token (no auth required)
CREATE POLICY "Public read via share token" ON walk_sessions
One row every 10 seconds during an active walk. This is what the trusted contact web view reads to show the live pin on the map.
id UUID DEFAULT gen_random_uuid() PRIMARY KEY,
session_id UUID REFERENCES walk_sessions(id) ON DELETE CASCADE NOT NULL,
user_id UUID REFERENCES auth.users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE NOT NULL,
CREATE INDEX ON location_pings (session_id, recorded_at DESC);
-- RLS: owner can read/write, public can read if session share_token valid
ALTER TABLE location_pings ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
CREATE POLICY "Owner manages pings" ON location_pings
CREATE POLICY "Public read via active session" ON location_pings
Audit log of every escalation action. This is used by the escalation ladder UI to show the user which stages have been triggered.
id UUID DEFAULT gen_random_uuid() PRIMARY KEY,
session_id UUID REFERENCES walk_sessions(id) ON DELETE CASCADE NOT NULL,
user_id UUID REFERENCES auth.users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE NOT NULL,
stage INTEGER NOT NULL CHECK (stage BETWEEN 1 AND 5),
ALTER TABLE escalation_events ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
CREATE POLICY "Users manage own escalations" ON escalation_events
In the Supabase dashboard, configure the following before writing any auth code:
Enable Email provider. Turn on "Confirm email" — users must verify before accessing the app.
Set Site URL to your Vercel deployment URL (update this when deploying).
Customize the confirmation email subject: "Confirm your SafeWalk account"
Set password minimum length to 8 characters.
Disable all OAuth providers for MVP (add Google OAuth in v1.1 if needed).
Two Edge Functions handle server-side operations that require secret keys (Twilio). Create these in supabase/functions/.
Called when escalation Stage 2 triggers. Accepts session_id and contact_ids, fetches the session location, and sends SMS via Twilio to each contact with the share link.
Reads share_token from walk_sessions, builds URL: https://your-app.vercel.app/track/{share_token}
Sends SMS via Twilio with location link and user's name
Logs each send as an escalation_event
Called at Stage 3. Makes an outbound voice call to the primary contact via Twilio with a pre-recorded TwiML message.
Uses Twilio Programmable Voice to call the primary contact's phone number
TwiML says: "This is SafeWalk. [User name] may need help. Their last known location has been sent to your phone."
4. Auth Flows — Build Before Any Other UI
Auth is the first thing to build. Every other page depends on knowing whether the user is signed in. Get auth fully working and tested before touching the home screen or walk logic.
The router has three categories of routes. Implement this structure first.
Redirect here after first sign-in if onboarding not completed

Implement a ProtectedRoute component that reads the Zustand auth store. On mount, call supabase.auth.getSession() to restore the session from localStorage. While loading, show a full-screen SafeWalk logo spinner — never a blank white flash.
Form fields: Full name, Email, Password. Validation with Zod: name required, email valid format, password minimum 8 characters.

On submit, call supabase.auth.signUp() with email, password, and options.data = { full_name }. The trigger in 3.2 will auto-create the profile row. On success, redirect to /auth/check-email (a static page that says "Check your email and click the confirmation link").
Do not auto-sign-in after signUp. The user must confirm their email first.
The onboarding_completed flag is false by default — users see onboarding after first confirmation.
Form fields: Email, Password. Link to /forgot-password. Link to /sign-up.
On submit, call supabase.auth.signInWithPassword(). On success, check profiles table for onboarding_completed boolean. If false, redirect to /onboarding. If true, redirect to /home.
Error states to handle: "Invalid login credentials" (show inline form error), "Email not confirmed" (show specific message with a resend link).

Single field: Email. On submit, call supabase.auth.resetPasswordForEmail() with redirect URL set to https://your-app.vercel.app/reset-password. Show a success state regardless of whether the email exists (security best practice — do not confirm whether an account exists).

Supabase sends the user here with a token in the URL hash. On page mount, call supabase.auth.getSession() — Supabase automatically exchanges the token for a session. Show a new password form. On submit, call supabase.auth.updateUser({ password: newPassword }). On success, redirect to /home.

This is the redirect URL after email confirmation. On mount, call supabase.auth.exchangeCodeForSession(). Then check onboarding_completed. Redirect to /onboarding (first time) or /home (returning user).

Initialize the Supabase client in src/lib/supabase.ts with persistSession: true (default). In your root App component, call supabase.auth.onAuthStateChange() and update the Zustand auth store on every event (SIGNED_IN, SIGNED_OUT, TOKEN_REFRESHED). This ensures the session survives page refreshes.
Shown exactly once, immediately after email confirmation. Three steps. After Step 3, set onboarding_completed = true in the profiles table and redirect to /home.
Gate the onboarding route: if onboarding_completed is already true, redirect to /home immediately.
Store the current step in local component state, not the URL — users should not be able to deep-link into step 2 directly.

Full-screen purple-tinted illustration with the SafeWalk logo. Headline: "Stay safe while you walk." Body: "SafeWalk monitors your journey and alerts your trusted contacts if something seems wrong." A single Next button advances to Step 2. Progress dots at the bottom (active pill, two inactive dots).

Headline: "Add your trusted contacts." Body: "Choose up to 5 people who'll be notified if you need help. They don't need the app." Show the AddContactForm inline (name, phone, email, is_primary toggle). A primary call to action: "Add contact and continue". Secondary ghost button: "Skip for now".

If they add a contact here, write it to the trusted_contacts table immediately. Do not defer to later. If they skip, show a persistent banner on the home screen: "Add a trusted contact to activate safety alerts" until they do.

A modal-style overlay (not a system dialog). Headline: "Allow location access." Body: "SafeWalk needs your location only while a walk is active. We never track you in the background." A purple info box: "Location is only active during walks and deleted after 30 days." Primary button: "Allow location" — this triggers the browser's native geolocation permission prompt. If denied, show a graceful fallback: "You can enable location later in your browser settings."
This is the app's main screen. It has two states: idle (no active walk) and active (walk in progress). The map is always visible. The bottom sheet changes between states.

Full-viewport layout. The map fills the entire screen from top to bottom. A top bar (non-sticky, just a visual layer) shows the SafeWalk logo on the left and the user's avatar (initials circle) on the right — tapping the avatar goes to /settings. The bottom sheet slides up from the bottom and sits above the map.
The map is always rendered — it does not unmount when the walk starts.
The bottom sheet height changes between idle and active walk states.
Use CSS transform: translateY() with a transition for the sheet animation, not mounting/unmounting.
Initialize the Mapbox map in a MapView component. Configuration:
Style: mapbox://styles/mapbox/streets-v12 (clean street map, good readability)
Center: User's current location on mount. If location not available, center on Winnipeg, MB (default for MVP).
Controls: Only the geolocate control (blue dot button). Remove zoom buttons — they waste space on mobile.
User pin: A custom purple circle marker, not the default Mapbox marker. 12px diameter, white border 2px, purple fill #7F77DD.
Route polyline: Purple dashed line from current location to destination. Only show when destination is set.
ETA chip: Small white pill floating above the destination marker showing "8 min". Positioned at the midpoint of the route.
Sheet height: approximately 220px. Contents from top to bottom:
Sheet handle bar (32px wide, 3px tall, gray, centered)
Current location display: purple dot + address text + chevron right (tapping opens destination input)
Stats row: three metric cards side by side — "Walks this month" (count), "Contacts" (count), "Status" (Safe/Active badge)
Start Walk button — full width, purple background, white text, 48px tall, 12px border radius. This is the primary CTA.
Stats are pulled from Supabase: count of walk_sessions for current month, count of trusted_contacts, and current walk status.
When the user taps Start Walk:
Request geolocation permission if not already granted. If denied, show a toast: "Location access is needed to start a walk."
Create a new walk_sessions row in Supabase with status = "active", user_id = current user, started_at = now().
Store the session ID in Zustand walkStore.
Start the useGeolocation hook — watchPosition with maximumAge: 0, timeout: 10000, enableHighAccuracy: true.
Every 10 seconds, write a new location_pings row to Supabase.
Start the check-in countdown timer (90 seconds by default).
Animate the bottom sheet up to the active walk state.
Sheet height: approximately 280px. Contents from top to bottom:
Check-in countdown bar — full width, purple background, "Next check-in" label on the left, timer (MM:SS) on the right. This is always visible during a walk and updates every second.
Session info row: walk timer (HH:MM:SS, left), distance walked (left below timer), current street name (right), heading direction (right below), "All good" green badge (far right).
SOS zone: SOS button (large red circle, 64px, press-and-hold) on the left. To the right: "Press and hold to activate emergency" helper text above, "End walk" ghost button below.
When End Walk is tapped, show a confirmation dialog: "End this walk? Your session will be saved." Two buttons: Cancel and End walk. On confirm:
Set walk_sessions status = "completed", ended_at = now(), calculate duration_seconds and distance_meters.
Stop watchPosition and clear the Zustand walk state.
Show a success toast: "Walk saved. Walked 1.2 km in 22 minutes."
Animate the bottom sheet back to idle state.
This is the core safety mechanism. Build it carefully. Every stage must be logged to escalation_events in Supabase.
The check-in countdown runs continuously during an active walk. When it hits zero, Stage 1 of the escalation ladder begins. The countdown resets to 90 seconds when:
The user taps "I'm okay" during a check-in prompt
The user manually triggers a check-in from the walk screen
Implement this in a useCheckIn hook. Store the countdown value in Zustand so it can drive the UI timer and trigger escalation from anywhere in the component tree.
When the countdown reaches zero, execute this sequence. Each stage only proceeds if the previous stage did not get resolved (user responded).
Show full-screen "Are you okay?" overlay with countdown and "I'm okay" button. Status bar turns amber.
Call send-alert Edge Function. SMS sent to ALL trusted contacts with share link. Log escalation_events.
Call initiate-call Edge Function. Automated voice call to primary contact only.
Show prominent "Call 911" button with user's current address pre-filled. Cannot auto-dial 911.
Continue location pings. Status remains escalating. Walk session status = "escalating".
When Stage 1 triggers, display a full-screen overlay. Do NOT show this as a small card — it must be unmissable.
Headline: "Are you okay?" in large bold text
Body: "We haven't heard from you. Tap to confirm you're safe."
Small text below: "Your contacts will be alerted if no response"
Escalation ladder visible below — showing all 5 stages with done/active/pending state dots
At any stage, tapping "I'm okay" resolves the current escalation:
Set resolved = true on all open escalation_events for the session
Reset the check-in countdown to 90 seconds
Show toast: "Great, glad you're safe. Check-in reset."

The SOS button is a large red circle (64px diameter) with a white ring around it (3px, red). The ring creates visual separation from the map. Label: "SOS" in white, 12px bold. Position: left side of the active walk sheet's bottom zone.
The SOS button must NOT trigger on a simple tap. Implement press-and-hold:
On pointerdown: start a 3-second countdown. Show an animated progress ring around the SOS circle filling up.
Display countdown overlay: "3... 2... 1..." in the center.
If the user releases before 3 seconds (pointerup or pointercancel): cancel the countdown, reset the button to normal state.
If 3 seconds complete: SOS is activated. Skip to Stage 2 immediately (bypass the 30-second check-in window). This is an intentional emergency.
Use pointer events (not mouse/touch separately) so it works on both desktop and mobile.
Add a subtle vibration on supported devices: navigator.vibrate([100, 50, 100, 50, 200])
The progress ring must be visible — use an SVG stroke-dashoffset animation on a circle element.
Walk session status becomes "sos_triggered" in Supabase
Stage 2 escalation fires immediately (send-alert Edge Function called)
Show the escalation ladder overlay so the user sees what has been triggered
The "I'm okay" button is visible and prominent — allow cancellation at any point
Display all trusted_contacts for the current user. Each contact row shows:
Avatar circle with initials (purple background, purple-dark text)
"Primary" badge (purple pill) on the is_primary contact — only one contact can be primary
Tap to expand and show edit/delete options
Below the list, show: "Add contact (X of 5 remaining)" — only show this if count < 5. If count = 5, show "Maximum contacts reached."

If no contacts exist, show a centered empty state with an icon, headline "No trusted contacts yet", body "Add at least one person who should be notified if you need help", and an "Add your first contact" button.
Opens as a bottom sheet or modal. Fields:
Phone number (required) — validate Canadian/US format, store with country code
"Set as primary contact" toggle — if toggled, unset any existing primary before saving
On save: POST to trusted_contacts via Supabase. Show success toast: "[Name] added as a trusted contact." Refresh the contacts list.

Show a confirmation dialog: "Remove [Name] as a trusted contact? They won't be notified during future walks." Two buttons: Cancel and Remove. On confirm, DELETE from trusted_contacts. If deleting the primary contact, show a warning: "Please set a new primary contact."
This is a PUBLIC route — no authentication required. A trusted contact opens this URL from an SMS and sees the user's live location. This page must work on any mobile browser with no login prompt.
This page will be opened by people who have never heard of SafeWalk.
It must immediately communicate: who is sharing, that they may need help, and where they are.
It must load in under 2 seconds on a 4G mobile connection.
The page has no nav bar and no auth UI. Layout from top to bottom:
Purple header bar with SafeWalk logo (small, left) and title "SafeWalk — Live location". Below that, a subtitle: "[User's name] started a walk · shared with you."
Map — takes up approximately 40% of the viewport. Shows the user's last known location pin in purple. Auto-centers on the pin.
Info rows — a simple list of: Last updated (with timestamp and "auto-refreshing" note), Location (nearest address), Heading + speed, Walk started (time), Link expires (24h countdown).
Two call buttons side by side: "Call [Name]" (purple) and "Call 911" (red). The first opens the contact's phone number in tel: protocol. The second opens tel:911.
On mount, fetch the walk_session where share_token = the URL param. Validate that share_expires_at is in the future. If expired, show: "This link has expired. The walk has ended."
Then fetch the latest location_ping for that session_id. Display it. Set a 15-second interval to re-fetch and update the map pin. Show a small "Updated just now" / "Updated X seconds ago" indicator.

If the token is not found or expired, show a full-page state: SafeWalk logo, headline "This link has expired", body "Walk safety links are active for 24 hours. If you're concerned, contact [name] or call emergency services.", and a large "Call 911" button.
A chronological list of the user's past walk sessions, most recent first. Each row shows:
Destination or "Walk" if no destination was set
Status badge: green "Completed", amber "SOS used", gray "Ended early"
Tapping a row expands it to show: distance walked, starting address, and a small static map thumbnail (Mapbox Static Images API — no interactive map needed here).
Pagination: load 20 rows at a time. "Load more" button at the bottom.
Empty state: "No walks yet. Start your first walk to see your history here." with a Start Walk button.
At the top: avatar circle (initials), full name, email. An Edit link opens an inline edit mode for name and phone number. Calls supabase.auth.updateUser() for email, profiles table for name/phone.
"Check-in reminders" — whether to show in-app countdown prominently (default: on)
"Walk summary" — whether to show a summary toast when a walk ends (default: on)
Store these in the profiles table as JSON preferences column. No push notifications in web MVP — that requires a service worker and is a v1.1 feature.
"Location only during walks" — informational toggle (always on, not editable). Shows users that background tracking is never used.
"Auto-delete after 30 days" — toggle. When enabled, walk_sessions and location_pings older than 30 days are deleted via a Supabase scheduled Edge Function (cron). Default: on.

"Export my data" — generates and downloads a JSON file containing the user's profile, contacts, and walk history. Implement as a client-side function that fetches all three tables and calls JSON.stringify + creates a Blob download.

"Delete all my data" — shows a confirmation dialog requiring the user to type "DELETE" to confirm. On confirm: delete from trusted_contacts, walk_sessions (cascades to location_pings and escalation_events), then call supabase.auth.admin.deleteUser() via an Edge Function. Sign out and redirect to /sign-in.
At the bottom: app version (v1.0.0), "PIPEDA compliant" note, "Sign out" link.
Build these base components in src/components/ui/ before building any page. Every page uses them.
variant: primary | ghost | danger | link. size: sm | md | lg. loading: boolean (shows spinner, disables). fullWidth: boolean.
label, error, helperText. Passes all standard input props. Shows red border and error text on invalid state.
variant: success | warning | danger | info | purple. size: sm | md. Pill shape.
initials: string. size: sm | md | lg. Background: purple-light, text: purple-dark.
isOpen, onClose, title, children. Prevents background scroll. Closes on Escape key.
isOpen, onClose, children. Slides up from bottom. 60% screen height max. Draggable handle.
Uses react-hot-toast. Helper functions: toast.success(), toast.error(), toast.info(). Purple accent on success.
size: sm | md | lg. Purple color. Used inside Button loading state and full-page loading.
icon, title, body, actionLabel, onAction. Centered layout. Used on all empty list pages.
title, body, confirmLabel, confirmVariant, onConfirm, onCancel. Built on Modal.
Define all colors as Tailwind CSS custom config values. Never use arbitrary hex values in component files — always reference these tokens.

Typography: use Inter as the web font (import from Google Fonts). Font sizes: 12px (captions), 14px (body), 16px (input text), 18px (section headers), 22px (page titles). Line height: 1.6 for all body text.
Border radius: 8px (inputs, badges), 12px (cards, sheets), 50% (avatars, SOS button). Border width: always 0.5px or 1px, never thicker except the SOS ring (3px).
This is a web app that must feel native on a phone. Build mobile-first.
All touch targets: minimum 48px height and 48px width (WCAG 2.5.5).
The bottom sheet and nav bar must be thumb-reachable — keep critical actions in the bottom 40% of the viewport.
The SOS button must be reachable one-handed. Position it center-left of the bottom sheet.
No hover-only interactions — everything must work on touch.
Font size minimum 14px — never smaller, even for captions.
Add a viewport meta tag: <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1, maximum-scale=1">
Add a PWA manifest (public/manifest.json) with name, short_name, icons, theme_color (#7F77DD), and display: standalone. This lets users add SafeWalk to their home screen.
Follow this order. Each phase should be working and tested before moving to the next.
Project setup, Tailwind, Supabase client, env vars, all database tables + RLS, UI component library (Button, Input, Badge, Avatar, Modal)
All tables exist in Supabase with RLS. UI components render correctly.
Sign up, sign in, forgot password, reset password, auth callback, ProtectedRoute, session persistence
Full auth cycle works: sign up → email → confirm → sign in → sign out → reset password.
Three-step onboarding flow, contacts form, location permission step, onboarding_completed flag
New user completes onboarding, is_onboarded set to true, redirected to home.
MapView, user pin, idle bottom sheet, stats cards, Start Walk button, geolocation hook, location ping writes to Supabase
User can start a walk, map shows real location, pings appear in Supabase table.
Active walk sheet, session timer, check-in countdown bar, End Walk flow, walk_sessions writes
Walk session created on start, updated on end, history shows the completed walk.
useCheckIn hook, check-in overlay, escalation ladder UI, escalation_events writes, Stage 1 full-screen prompt
Check-in timer reaches zero, overlay appears, "I'm okay" resets it.
Press-and-hold SOS button, progress ring animation, Stage 2 skip on SOS, escalation ladder overlay
Press and hold for 3s triggers SOS. Escalation ladder shows stages fired.
Supabase Edge Functions (send-alert, initiate-call), Twilio integration, SMS with share link
SOS fires SMS to a test contact with a working share link.
Public /track/:token page, share link fetching, 15s auto-refresh, call buttons, expired state
Opening the share link shows live map pin. Pin updates every 15 seconds.
Contact list, add form, delete with confirm, empty state, primary badge
User can add, view, and delete contacts. Primary contact designated.
Walk history list, session rows, badges, expand to detail, load more pagination
Completed walks appear in history with correct duration and status.
Profile edit, notification toggles, data export, delete account, sign out, privacy page
User can export data, delete account, and sign out.
Loading states, error states, empty states, toast messages, Lighthouse audit, PWA manifest
Lighthouse score 90+ on performance, 100 on accessibility. No console errors.
Every async operation needs three states handled: loading, success, and error. Never show a blank screen.
Inline form error below the field
Button spinner for 1-2s while Supabase writes
Red error state in ladder row: "SMS failed. Retrying..."
Full-page error with "Call 911" button still visible
Error banner: "Couldn't load contacts. Pull to refresh."
SafeWalk must be usable by people with motor and visual impairments. Key requirements:
All interactive elements have ARIA labels. The SOS button: aria-label="Emergency SOS — press and hold to activate".
Color is never the only way to convey information. Badges have both a color and a text label.
Focus is trapped inside modals and bottom sheets when open.
Keyboard navigation works for all flows (tab, enter, escape).
The SOS button is reachable via keyboard (spacebar activates, then hold logic is simulated with a timer).
All images and icons have alt text or aria-hidden if decorative.
The check-in countdown announces to screen readers every 10 seconds via an aria-live="polite" region.
Minimum contrast ratio 4.5:1 for all text. Purple #7F77DD on white passes — verify with a contrast checker.
RLS enabled on every table — verify by attempting to access another user's data from the Supabase SQL editor.
Twilio credentials are server-side only — in Edge Functions, never in client code.
Mapbox token is scoped to your domain only — set allowed URLs in the Mapbox dashboard.
Share tokens are UUID v4 — not sequential, not guessable.
Share links expire after 24 hours — verified server-side in the RLS policy, not just client-side.
No sensitive data logged to the console in production — use an environment check before any console.log calls.
Email confirmation is required before any data access — verified by Supabase auth session.
HTTPS only — enforced by Vercel automatically.
Connect the GitHub repository to Vercel. Configure:
All environment variables from .env.local added to Vercel project settings (not the TWILIO_ ones — those go in Supabase Edge Function secrets).
Set the Supabase Site URL and redirect URL to the Vercel deployment URL after first deploy.
Add a vercel.json with a rewrite rule so all routes return index.html (enables React Router):
After deployment, test the full flow on a real mobile device (not just browser DevTools mobile emulation) before considering the MVP complete.
End of SafeWalk Web MVP Build Specification
